changed_functions.name: CCFFilterAppInstanceEA
key_changes: Single-byte patch at 0x1c00099f7: conditional branch opcode changed from jb (0x0f82, jump-if-below) to ja (0x0f87, jump-if-above), reversing the bounds check direction for EaValueLength validation against 0x14 (20 bytes).

UNPATCHED: The error path (0xC0000053) is only reached when EaValueLength > 0x14. Values 0x00-0x0F, 0x11-0x13 fall through and trigger a 20-byte read from the EA value, exceeding the actual EaValueLength.
PATCHED: The error path is correctly reached when EaValueLength < 0x14. Only values >= 0x14 fall through to the 20-byte read path, ensuring the buffer is large enough.
changed_functions.security_notes: This is an inverted bounds check (CWE-697: Incorrect Comparison). The function CCFFilterAppInstanceEA parses FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entries looking for the 'ClusteredApplicationInstance' EA. When found, it reads the EA value: 16 bytes if EaValueLength==0x10 (GUID only), or 20 bytes (GUID + 4-byte version) for the 'large' path. The bounds check that gates the 20-byte read path is inverted: the unpatched code uses jb (jump-if-below), rejecting only EaValueLength > 0x14, when it should reject EaValueLength < 0x14. This causes an out-of-bounds read of up to 20 bytes from kernel pool memory when an attacker supplies a crafted EA with EaValueLength between 0x00-0x0F or 0x11-0x13. The OOB data is copied into the AppInstance GUID (16 bytes via movups/movdqu at [r14]) and a version field (4 bytes via mov dword [rax], edx). Back in CCFPreCreate, the 16-byte GUID is copied into a GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE extra create parameter (allocated by FltAllocateExtraCreateParameter at 0x1c0007ac2, filled at 0x1c0007b90, attached via CCFAddEcpToCreate at 0x1c0007baa) on the create IRP; the 4-byte version is written to a separate local output. Impact: denial of service (BSOD if the 20-byte read crosses into an unmapped page) and kernel information exposure (adjacent pool bytes moved into the ECP; attacker-side retrieval of the ECP is not demonstrated). Additionally, strncmp is called with EaNameLength as the max comparison length; setting EaNameLength=0 causes a trivial match (strncmp returns 0 for n=0), enabling exploitation even with a minimal EA entry.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- Key vulnerable block (unpatched, 0x1c00099ef-0x1c0009a14) ---
0x1c00099ef: mov     eax, 0x14           ; load 20 (expected min EaValueLength)
0x1c00099f4: cmp     ax, dx              ; compare 0x14 vs EaValueLength (dx)
0x1c00099f7: jb      0x1c0009aa0         ; BUG: jump to error only if 0x14 < EaValueLength
; ^^^ UNPATCHED uses 0f82 (jb); PATCHED uses 0f87 (ja) ^^^
; Fall-through (buggy path) reads 20 bytes regardless of EaValueLength:
0x1c00099fd: movzx   eax, byte [rbx+0x5]      ; eax = EaNameLength (attacker-controlled)
0x1c0009a01: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rax+rbx+0x9]  ; OOB READ: 16 bytes from EaValue start
0x1c0009a06: movdqu  xmmword [r14], xmm0         ; store leaked bytes to output GUID
0x1c0009a0b: mov     edx, dword [rax+rbx+0x19]   ; OOB READ: 4 more bytes at EaValue+16
0x1c0009a0f: mov     rax, qword [rsp+0x70]
0x1c0009a14: mov     dword [rax], edx            ; store leaked bytes to output version

security_findings.entry_point: NtCreateFile / CreateFileW with ExtendedAttributes (EaBuffer) containing a crafted 'ClusteredApplicationInstance' EA entry, on a volume managed by the ccffilter.sys minifilter (Cluster Shared Volumes / CSV)
call_chain: NtCreateFile / CreateFileW (user mode) with EaBuffer parameter
I/O Manager dispatches IRP_MJ_CREATE through filter stack
FltMgr invokes CCFPreCreate (Pre-operation callback for IRP_MJ_CREATE, registered via FltRegisterFilter)
CCFFilterAppInstanceEA at 0x1c000990c walks FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION chain, matches 'ClusteredApplicationInstance'
Inverted bounds check at 0x1c00099f7 (jb instead of ja) allows OOB read when EaValueLength < 0x14
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Out-of-bounds read due to inverted bounds check (CWE-125 / CWE-697)
security_findings.description: CCFFilterAppInstanceEA parses FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entries from a user-supplied EaBuffer attached to an IRP_MJ_CREATE request. When it finds an EA named 'ClusteredApplicationInstance', it reads the EA value data. There are two valid value sizes: 0x10 bytes (16-byte GUID) and >= 0x14 bytes (20-byte GUID + 4-byte version). The bounds check at 0x1c00099f7 that validates whether the EA value is large enough for the 20-byte read path is INVERTED: it uses 'jb' (jump-if-below), which sends EaValueLength > 0x14 to the error path, but allows EaValueLength <= 0x14 (including values 0-15 and 17-19) to fall through and trigger a 20-byte movups+dword read from the EA value buffer. Since the actual value data may be as small as 0 bytes, this reads up to 20 bytes beyond the valid EA value into adjacent kernel pool memory. The leaked bytes are stored to the output GUID (arg4, 16 bytes at [r14]) and version (arg5, 4 bytes via [rsp+0x70] dereference). Back in CCFPreCreate, the 16-byte GUID is copied into a GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE extra create parameter (allocated by FltAllocateExtraCreateParameter, attached via CCFAddEcpToCreate) on the create IRP.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. The target volume must be managed by ccffilter.sys (Windows Cluster Shared Volumes filter). This is a Windows Server Failover Clustering component.
2. The attacker calls NtCreateFile/CreateFileW with an EaBuffer containing a FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entry where:
   - EaName starts with 'ClusteredApplicationInstance' (or EaNameLength is set to 0, causing strncmp to trivially match)
   - EaValueLength is set to a value that is NOT 0x10 and is LESS THAN 0x14 (e.g., 0x00, 0x01, 0x02, ..., 0x0F, 0x11, 0x12, 0x13)
   - The actual EaValue data following the EA name is shorter than 20 bytes
3. This causes the inverted check (jb at 0x1c00099f7) to NOT jump to the error path, falling through to the 20-byte movups read that exceeds the actual value size.
4. To maximize OOB read distance: set EaValueLength=0 (no value data at all) and place this as the last/only EA entry so the read extends into adjacent pool allocations.
security_findings.primitive:
1. Kernel information disclosure: Up to 20 bytes of kernel pool memory adjacent to the EA buffer allocation are read out of bounds into the AppInstance GUID (16 bytes) and version (4 bytes); the 16-byte GUID is then copied into a GUID_ECP_NETWORK_APP_INSTANCE ECP attached to the create IRP.
2. Denial of service (BSOD): If the 20-byte read crosses a page boundary into an unmapped page, the kernel crashes with an access violation. This is the most reliable exploitation outcome.
3. Amplification via EaNameLength manipulation: Setting EaNameLength=0 causes strncmp(name, 'ClusteredApplicationInstance', 0) to return 0 (trivial match per C standard), reducing the EA entry to just the 8-byte header. The 20-byte read then starts at rbx+9, immediately past the header, maximizing OOB distance into adjacent memory.
security_findings.assembly:
CCFFilterAppInstanceEA @ 0x1c000990c (ccffilter_unpatched.sys) — 156 instructions, 34 basic blocks

=== Full function assembly (key blocks annotated) ===

; Prologue
0x1c000990c: mov     rax, rsp
0x1c000990f: mov     qword [rax+0x8], rbx
0x1c0009913: mov     qword [rax+0x10], rbp
0x1c0009917: mov     qword [rax+0x18], rsi
0x1c000991b: mov     qword [rax+0x20], rdi
0x1c000991f: push    r12
0x1c0009921: push    r14
0x1c0009923: push    r15
0x1c0009925: sub     rsp, 0x30
0x1c0009929: mov     rbx, qword [rcx]      ; rbx = *arg1 = pointer to first FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION
0x1c000992c: xor     edi, edi              ; rdi = 0 (status = STATUS_SUCCESS)
0x1c000992e: mov     r15d, edi
0x1c0009931: mov     ebp, edi
0x1c0009933: mov     sil, dil
0x1c0009936: mov     r14, r9               ; r14 = arg4 (output GUID buffer)
0x1c0009939: mov     r12, rdx              ; r12 = arg2 (EaLength pointer)

; WPP tracing check (skipped)
0x1c000993c: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0004088]
0x1c0009943: lea     rax, [rel 0x1c0004088]
0x1c000994a: cmp     rcx, rax
0x1c000994d: je      0x1c0009968

; === EA NAME COMPARISON LOOP ===
0x1c0009968: movzx   r8d, byte [rbx+0x5]   ; r8d = EaNameLength
0x1c000996d: lea     rcx, [rbx+0x8]        ; rcx = EaName (offset 8)
0x1c0009971: lea     rdx, [rel 0x1c00032a8] ; rdx = "ClusteredApplicationInstance"
0x1c0009978: call    qword [rel 0x1c00060e0] ; strncmp(EaName, "ClusteredApplicationInstance", EaNameLength)
0x1c000997f: nop
0x1c0009984: test    eax, eax
0x1c0009986: je      0x1c000999c            ; if match (==0), process EA value

; No match: advance to next EA entry
0x1c0009988: mov     eax, dword [rbx]       ; eax = NextEntryOffset
0x1c000998a: test    eax, eax
0x1c000998c: je      0x1c0009ad5            ; if 0, end of list
0x1c0009992: add     ebp, eax               ; accumulate offset
0x1c0009994: mov     r15, rbx
0x1c0009997: add     rbx, rax               ; rbx = next EA entry
0x1c000999a: jmp     0x1c0009968            ; loop back

; === EA VALUE SIZE CHECK ===
0x1c000999c: movzx   edx, word [rbx+0x6]    ; edx = EaValueLength (16-bit at offset 6)
0x1c00099a0: mov     eax, 0x10              ; check if == 16 (GUID-only format)
0x1c00099a5: cmp     ax, dx
0x1c00099a8: jne     0x1c00099ef            ; if != 0x10, go to 20-byte check

; --- 0x10 (16-byte GUID) path ---
0x1c00099aa: movzx   eax, byte [rbx+0x5]    ; eax = EaNameLength
0x1c00099ae: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rax+rbx+0x9]  ; read 16 bytes from EaValue
0x1c00099b3: movdqu  xmmword [r14], xmm0    ; store to output GUID (arg4)
; ... WPP, set arg5=0, jump to shared cleanup ...

; --- THE VULNERABLE CHECK (EaValueLength != 0x10 path) ---
0x1c00099ef: mov     eax, 0x14              ; eax = 20 (minimum for GUID+version)
0x1c00099f4: cmp     ax, dx                 ; compare 0x14 vs EaValueLength
0x1c00099f7: jb      0x1c0009aa0            ; *** BUG: 0f82 (jb) — should be 0f87 (ja) ***
;                                           ; UNPATCHED: error only if EaValueLength > 0x14
;                                           ; PATCHED:   error if EaValueLength < 0x14

; --- Fall-through: 20-byte read (VULNERABLE when EaValueLength < 0x14) ---
0x1c00099fd: movzx   eax, byte [rbx+0x5]    ; eax = EaNameLength (attacker-controlled)
0x1c0009a01: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rax+rbx+0x9]  ; OOB READ 16 bytes from [rbx+EaNameLength+9]
0x1c0009a06: movdqu  xmmword [r14], xmm0    ; store to output GUID (arg4)
0x1c0009a0b: mov     edx, dword [rax+rbx+0x19]   ; OOB READ 4 bytes from [rbx+EaNameLength+25]
0x1c0009a0f: mov     rax, qword [rsp+0x70]  ; rax = arg5 (output version ptr)
0x1c0009a14: mov     dword [rax], edx       ; store to output version (arg5)
; ... WPP, jump to shared cleanup ...

; --- Error path (0xC0000053) ---
0x1c0009aa0: mov     edi, 0xc0000053        ; STATUS error code
; ... return with error ...

; === Shared cleanup: check if GUID is zero, compact EA buffer ===
0x1c0009a48: mov     rax, qword [r14]       ; check first 8 bytes of GUID
0x1c0009a4b: sub     rax, qword [rel 0x1c0003320]  ; compare against known value
0x1c0009a52: jne     0x1c0009a5f
0x1c0009a54: mov     rax, qword [r14+0x8]   ; check last 8 bytes of GUID
0x1c0009a58: sub     rax, qword [rel 0x1c0003328]
0x1c0009a5f: test    rax, rax
0x1c0009a62: mov     rax, qword [rsp+0x80]  ; arg7 (output flag)
0x1c0009a6a: setz    cl
0x1c0009a6d: mov     byte [rax], cl         ; set flag: GUID is zero?
0x1c0009a6f: mov     eax, dword [rbx]       ; NextEntryOffset
0x1c0009a71: test    eax, eax
0x1c0009a73: jne     0x1c0009a86            ; compact if more entries
0x1c0009a75: mov     dword [r12], ebp       ; set EaLength = accumulated
0x1c0009a79: test    r15, r15
0x1c0009a7c: je      0x1c0009a81
0x1c0009a7e: mov     dword [r15], edi       ; clear prev entry's NextEntryOffset
0x1c0009a81: mov     sil, 0x1               ; set found flag
0x1c0009a84: jmp     0x1c0009ad5            ; exit
0x1c0009a86: sub     dword [r12], eax       ; adjust EaLength
0x1c0009a8a: mov     rcx, rbx
0x1c0009a8d: mov     r8d, dword [r12]
0x1c0009a91: mov     edx, dword [rbx]
0x1c0009a93: sub     r8d, ebp
0x1c0009a96: add     rdx, rbx
0x1c0009a99: call    memmove                ; compact remaining entries
0x1c0009a9e: jmp     0x1c0009a81

; Epilogue
0x1c0009ad5: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0004088]
; ... WPP tracing ...
0x1c0009b17: mov     rcx, qword [rsp+0x78]  ; arg6
0x1c0009b1c: mov     eax, edi               ; return status
0x1c0009b1e: mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x50]  ; restore rbx
0x1c0009b23: mov     rbp, qword [rsp+0x58]  ; restore rbp
0x1c0009b28: mov     rdi, qword [rsp+0x68]  ; restore rdi
0x1c0009b2d: mov     byte [rcx], sil        ; *arg6 = found flag
0x1c0009b30: mov     rsi, qword [rsp+0x60]  ; restore rsi
0x1c0009b35: add     rsp, 0x30
0x1c0009b39: pop     r15
0x1c0009b3b: pop     r14
0x1c0009b3d: pop     r12
0x1c0009b3f: retn

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. rcx = arg1 (pointer to EaBuffer pointer, dereference to get rbx = FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION*). Inspect the EA chain to confirm attacker-supplied data. r9 = arg4 (output GUID buffer at [r14]), [rsp+0x28] = arg5 (output version pointer).

This is the vulnerable conditional branch (jb/ja). At this point: dx (lower 16 bits of rdx) = EaValueLength from [rbx+0x6], ax = 0x14. Watch whether the jump is taken. In the UNPATCHED binary, jb means the error path is only taken if EaValueLength > 0x14. Verify the bug fires by confirming fall-through with EaValueLength < 0x14.

The OOB read instruction: movups xmm0, xmmword [rax+rbx+0x9]. At this point rax = EaNameLength (byte at [rbx+0x5]), rbx = current EA entry base. The effective read address is rbx+EaNameLength+9 = start of EaValue. If EaValueLength < 0x14, this reads 16 bytes beyond valid data. Inspect [rax+rbx+0x9] to see how far beyond the valid EA value the read extends.

The call site in CCFPreCreate that invokes CCFFilterAppInstanceEA. arg1 (rcx) = &Iopb->Parameters.Create.EaBuffer, arg2 (rdx) = &Iopb->Parameters.Create.EaLength. Verify the EaBuffer contents and EaLength before stepping into the vulnerable function.

watch: dx register at 0x1c00099f4 — holds EaValueLength (word from [rbx+0x6]); values 0x00-0x0F, 0x11-0x13 trigger the bug (fall through to OOB read)
ax register at 0x1c00099f4 — holds 0x14 (20), the minimum expected EaValueLength
rax at 0x1c0009a01 — holds EaNameLength (byte from [rbx+0x5]); controls the read offset; value 0 causes strncmp trivial match and read from rbx+9
rbx register throughout — pointer to current FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entry; [rbx+0]=NextEntryOffset, [rbx+5]=EaNameLength, [rbx+6]=EaValueLength, [rbx+8]=EaName
[rax+rbx+0x9] at 0x1c0009a01 — effective OOB read source address; compare against end of EaBuffer allocation to confirm OOB distance
r14 register — output GUID buffer (arg4); the 16 leaked bytes land here
[rsp+0x70] at 0x1c0009a0f — arg5 pointer; the 4 leaked version bytes are stored here
key_offsets: 0x1c00099f7: The single patched byte — opcode 0x0f82 (jb) in unpatched vs 0x0f87 (ja) in patched. This is the entire vulnerability.
0x1c000999c: movzx edx, word [rbx+0x6] — loads EaValueLength from EA structure offset 6
0x1c00099a8: jne 0x1c00099ef — branch that separates the 0x10 (GUID-only) path from the 0x14 (GUID+version) path
0x1c0009a01: movups xmm0, [rax+rbx+0x9] — first OOB read (16 bytes) into the EA value area
0x1c0009a0b: mov edx, [rax+rbx+0x19] — second OOB read (4 bytes) at EA value + 0x10
0x1c0009aa0: mov edi, 0xc0000053 — error path (should be taken for EaValueLength < 0x14 but isn't in unpatched)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
1. Ensure the system is running the UNPATCHED ccffilter.sys (Cluster Shared Volumes filter driver, part of Windows Server Failover Clustering). The driver must be loaded and attached to a CSV volume.
2. From user mode, call NtCreateFile (or CreateFileW with FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS) targeting a file on a CSV-managed volume.
3. Supply an Extended Attributes buffer (EaBuffer) via the ExtendedAttributes parameter containing a single FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entry:
   - NextEntryOffset = 0 (last entry)
   - Flags = 0
   - EaNameLength = 0x1C (28, for 'ClusteredApplicationInstance')
   - EaValueLength = 0x01 (or any value 0x00-0x0F or 0x11-0x13, NOT 0x10 or >= 0x14)
   - EaName = 'ClusteredApplicationInstance\0' (28 chars + null)
   - EaValue = single byte (e.g., 0x41)
   Total EA entry size = 8 (header) + 29 (name+null) + 1 (value) = 38 bytes
4. The EaBuffer total size should be exactly 38 bytes (no padding) to maximize OOB read distance.
5. Alternatively, for maximal OOB: set EaNameLength = 0 (strncmp trivially matches), EaValueLength = 1, EaName = just '\0', EaValue = single byte. Total = 8 + 1 + 1 = 10 bytes. The read starts at rbx+9 and reads 20 bytes, going 11+ bytes beyond the buffer.
6. The create request triggers CCFPreCreate → CCFFilterAppInstanceEA → OOB read.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At breakpoint 0x1c00099f7: dx will show the crafted EaValueLength (e.g., 0x0001). ax = 0x0014. The jb tests ax < dx unsigned (0x14 < EaValueLength); with EaValueLength=0x01 this is false, so jb is NOT taken and execution falls through to the OOB read. Execution reaches 0x1c00099fd and performs movups from an address beyond the valid EA value. If using a minimal buffer (EaValueLength=1, buffer ends shortly after), the read extends 19 bytes past valid data into kernel pool. If the adjacent memory is unmapped or at a page boundary, the system BSODs with BUGCODE or PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA. If the read succeeds, the leaked bytes appear in the output GUID buffer at [r14] and can be observed in the ECP created by CCFPreCreate.
debugging_aids.notes: The EA structure layout (FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION): offset 0=NextEntryOffset(4), offset 4=Flags(1), offset 5=EaNameLength(1), offset 6=EaValueLength(2), offset 8=EaName(variable+null), then EaValue(variable). The read address [rbx+EaNameLength+9] points to the start of EaValue (past the null terminator). The additional read at [rbx+EaNameLength+0x19] is at EaValue+0x10 (16 bytes into the value). The strncmp bypass via EaNameLength=0 is per C standard (strncmp with n=0 returns 0). Note: ccffilter.sys image base may be ASLR-randomized at runtime; use 'lm ccffilter' or '.reload /f ccffilter.sys' to resolve symbols, then add the module base to the offsets above. The driver is typically loaded on Windows Server with Failover Clustering / Cluster Shared Volumes enabled.

summary: The patch fixes a single-byte inverted bounds check in CCFFilterAppInstanceEA (ccffilter.sys, the Windows Cluster Shared Volumes minifilter). The condition at 0x1c00099f7 was changed from jb (jump-if-below) to ja (jump-if-above), reversing the EaValueLength validation for the 20-byte EA value read path. In the unpatched version, when an attacker supplies a FILE_FULL_EA_INFORMATION entry named 'ClusteredApplicationInstance' with EaValueLength between 0x00-0x0F or 0x11-0x13, the code performs a 20-byte movups+dword read from the EA value buffer despite the actual value being shorter, causing an out-of-bounds read of up to 20 bytes from adjacent kernel pool memory. A PoC would call NtCreateFile/CreateFileW on a CSV-filtered volume with a crafted EaBuffer containing this malformed EA entry, triggering either a kernel information disclosure (leaked pool memory propagated through ECP) or a BSOD (page fault on unmapped memory).
